name: Central African Republic (CAR / République Centrafricaine)
summary: Central African Republic is a Central African civil-law unitary republic whose family-law framework operates under the Family Code 1997 (Code de la Famille, Law 97.013) drawing on French civil-law substantive heritage with codification of customary-law marriage provisions. Parental authority and child custody are governed by Family Code arts. 269-301. The Court of Cassation (Cour de Cassation) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the Constitutional Court (Cour Constitutionnelle) operates separate constitutional review. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Court of First Instance (Tribunal de Grande Instance). Psychology profession is regulated through the Ministry of Health framework. CAR is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the interest-of-the-child standard. CAR is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.title: Family Code — Parental authority and custody
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Family Code drawing on French civil-law substantive heritage with codification of customary-law marriage provisions. Arts. 269-301 govern parental authority and child custody.

apex_courts.name: Court of Cassation (Cour de Cassation)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Constitutional Court (Cour Constitutionnelle)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Court with original jurisdiction over constitutional review.

professional_regulators.name: Ministry of Health, CAR
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: CAR family-court decisions are anonymised per Court of Cassation practice using initials.

key_developments.title: CAR independence + Dacko presidency + Bokassa coup context
key_developments.description: Central African Republic achieved independence 13 August 1960 from France under David Dacko presidency 1960-1965. Jean-Bédel Bokassa 1966 coup beginning of substantively distinctive recurring-coup-d'état pattern. Bokassa self-proclaimed Emperor 1976-1979 — among the most-distinctive African autocratic regimes.

key_developments.title: CAR ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: CAR ratified the UNCRC on 23 April 1992 — among the early African ratifications globally. Framing the family-law-reform trajectory toward best-interests-of-the-child substantive doctrine within civil-law framework.

key_developments.title: Constitution 1995 + multi-party transition
key_developments.description: Constitution of the Central African Republic adopted 28 December 1994 by referendum (substantially amended 2004, 2016) — establishing semi-presidential republic framework. Substantively distinctive recurring constitutional-reform-and-political-instability pattern.

key_developments.title: Family Code 1997 (Law 97.013)
key_developments.description: Federal Family Code enacted 1997 (Law 97.013) drawing on French civil-law substantive heritage with codification of customary-law marriage provisions. Arts. 269-301 govern parental authority and child custody. Substantively significant family-law codification within Central African Francophone cluster.

key_developments.title: Bozizé coup d'état + recurring-coup pattern continuation
key_developments.description: François Bozizé coup d'état 15 March 2003 ended Ange-Félix Patassé presidency. Substantively distinctive recurring-coup-d'état pattern continuation: 2003, 2013, 2014 substantive political-institutional disruption affecting family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: CAR Civil War 2013-2015 + Séléka coup + Anti-balaka context
key_developments.description: Séléka coalition coup d'état 24 March 2013 ended Bozizé presidency. Subsequent Central African Republic Civil War 2013-2015 between Séléka (Muslim-dominant) and Anti-balaka (Christian-dominant) substantially disrupted political-institutional framework. UN MINUSCA peacekeeping mission established 2014.

key_developments.title: Constitution 2016 + Touadéra presidency + post-civil-war reconciliation
key_developments.description: Constitution of the Central African Republic adopted 30 March 2016 by referendum — substantively reforming political-institutional framework. Faustin-Archange Touadéra elected 14 February 2016. Substantive post-civil-war reconciliation and reconstruction trajectory affecting family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Wagner Group + Russian military involvement + geopolitical realignment
key_developments.description: Wagner Group Russian private military company arrived 2018 — substantively reshaping CAR's geopolitical positioning and military-security framework. Subsequent EU/Western disengagement and Russian-CAR partnership affecting cross-border-jurisdiction practice.

key_developments.title: Constitutional referendum 2023 + term-limit modifications + Touadéra entrenchment
key_developments.description: Constitutional referendum 30 July 2023 substantively reformed term-limit provisions enabling Touadéra's continued candidacy. Substantive contested constitutional reform affecting democratic-trajectory framework.

key_developments.title: Russia-Wagner framework + CEMAC + UN MINUSCA continuing-mission
key_developments.description: Central African Republic Russia-Wagner framework alignment 2022 substantively reshaping geopolitical framework affecting institutional context. CEMAC (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale) framework substantively significant cross-border family-law adjudication. UN MINUSCA continuing-mission framework substantively significant for institutional capacity. Haut Conseil de la Communication and Comité National des Droits de l'Homme institutional positions addressing PA-related issues within Family Code framework. Substantively significant Central African institutional context.

key_developments.title: Cour de Cassation + Cour Constitutionnelle — interest-of-the-child substantive register in constrained operational context
key_developments.description: Cour de Cassation and Cour Constitutionnelle continue to develop interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Family Code arts. 269-301 framework in custody disputes within constrained operational context of ongoing armed-group activity and post-Wagner military-security framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: CAR operates a French-civil-law family-law framework with codification of customary-law marriage provisions — places CAR in the Central African Francophone civil-law cluster with French-derivative substantive heritage.
Bilingual official-language framework (French + Sango) reflects post-independence language-policy heritage.
Non-Hague Convention status places CAR in the non-Hague Central African cluster.
editorial_notes: Central African Republic jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-08 from 1 to 10 key_developments with full Dacko-Bokassa-to-Touadéra trajectory: 1960-CAR-independence-+-Dacko-presidency-+-Bokassa-coup-context + 1992-UNCRC-ratification + 1995-Constitution-+-multi-party-transition + 1997-Family-Code-Law-97.013 + 2003-Bozizé-coup-d'état + 2013-CAR-Civil-War-+-Séléka-coup-+-Anti-balaka + 2016-Constitution-+-Touadéra-presidency-+-post-civil-war-reconciliation + 2018-Wagner-Group-Russian-military + 2023-Constitutional-referendum-+-term-limit-modifications + 2025-Cour-de-Cassation-+-Cour-Constitutionnelle-interest-of-the-child-constrained-context.
Civil-law Central African Francophone (Family Code 1997 + customary-law-codified + Constitution 2016 + non-Hague Convention + bilingual French-Sango).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive interest-of-the-child analysis under Family Code arts. 269-301 framework within constrained operational context without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Central-African + Francophone + civil-law + French-derivative + customary-law-codified + Bokassa-Emperor-1976-1979-globally-distinctive + recurring-coup-d'état-pattern-1966-2013 + CAR-Civil-War-2013-2015-Séléka-Anti-balaka + post-2018-Wagner-Group-Russian-military-geopolitical-realignment + non-Hague-Convention clusters within the corpus.
